text: CA 002/2025 (01) Olsen (02) Obed v Othmar SEPTEMBER 09, 2025 COURT OF APPEAL - ORDERS
IN THE DUBAI INTERNATIONAL FINANCIAL CENTRE COURT
IN THE COURT OF APPEAL
(01) OLSEN (02) OBED
Judgment Creditors/Appellants
and
OTHMAR
Second Judgment Debtor/Respondent
ORDER WITH REASONS OF H.E. CHIEF JUSTICE WAYNE MARTIN

UPON the Order with Reasons of H.E. Chief Justice Wayne Martin dated 26 June 2025 ordering the Second Judgment Debtor/Respondent to pay the Judgment Creditor/Appellants’ costs of the Appeal (the “26 June Order”)
AND UPON the Registry’s email dated 5 August 2025 issuing directions for the fi ling of submissions on costs
AND UPON the Judgment Creditors’ Statement of Costs dated 13 August 2025
AND UPON the Judgment Creditors’ costs submissions dated 22 August 2025
AND UPON the Second Judgment Debtor’s costs submissions dated 22 August 2025
AND UPON the Judgment Creditors’ submissions in reply dated 3 September 2025
IT IS HEREBY ORDERED THAT:
Claim No: CA 002/2025
BETWEEN
1. Pursuant to Order 7 of the 26 June Order, the costs to be paid to the Judgment Creditors/Appellants are assessed in the amount of AED 116,574.60.

2. If the costs are not paid in full within 14 days of the date of these orders, interest will be payable on any amount outstanding thereafter accruing at the rate of $5 \%$ per annum, compounded annually.

Issued by:   
Hayley Norton   
Assistant Registrar   
Date of issue: 9 September 2025   
At: 1pm
SCHEDULE OF REASONS
1. The Judgment Creditors/Appellants claim costs of AED 209,972.85 in respect of their costs of the Appeal which was dismissed in the 26 June Order because the issues in the Appeal had become moot.
2. Having regard to the following:
(e) on 25 March 2025 when the Dubai Court dismissed the Judgment Debtor/Respondent's claim it was inevitable that the appeal against the stay would become moot, as it did on 11 April 2025;
(f) the Appeal was never listed for hearing; and
(g) the Claim is for a little under 90 hours of professional time spent largely working on documents the costs claimed are excessive and disproportionate.
3. Costs will be assessed in the amount of AED 116,574.60, made up as to AED 26,574.60 and professional cost of AED 90,000.
4. Interest will accrue on any portion of the costs not paid within 14 days of the date of this order at the rate of $5 \%$ per annum, being the rate requested.
